commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: And Paul dwelt two whole years in his own hired house, and received all that came in unto him, Two whole years in his own hired lodging. That is, in the lodgings which St. Paul was permitted to hire for himself, and to live there, with a soldier chained to him for his guard. Happy soldier, if he knew how to make use of such a favourable opportunity. We may take notice by all this narration of St. Luke, (as when he says here, ver. 16, when we arrived at Rome) that he was all the way in the ship with St.

advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
